guideline: Use E11.9 when no specific complications (retinopathy, neuropathy, etc.) are documented. Ensure glycemic control status is noted in the record.
Assign when provider documents uncontrolled or poorly controlled T2DM with hyperglycemia. Do not use 'uncontrolled' as a standalone — specificity (hyperglycemia vs. hypoglycemia) is required.

description: Type 2 diabetes mellitus with diabetic neuropathy, unspecified
guideline: Use when neuropathy is linked to T2DM but the type is not specified. Query provider for peripheral, autonomic, or mononeuropathy for a more precise code.
Assign for high blood pressure not attributed to a secondary cause. If heart failure or CKD coexists, check for combination codes I11, I12, or I13.

description: Atherosclerotic heart disease of native coronary artery without angina pectoris
guideline: Use for CAD without angina. If angina is present, use I25.110 (unstable) or I25.119 (unspecified). Document whether vessels are native or grafted.
Avoid this code when possible — query for systolic vs. diastolic, acute vs. chronic. Specific HF codes (I50.20–I50.43) impact DRG assignment significantly.
Use when AFib type is not documented. Query for paroxysmal (I48.0), persistent (I48.11/I48.19), long-standing persistent (I48.20), or chronic (I48.21).
Assign when no status asthmaticus or exacerbation is noted. Document severity (mild intermittent, moderate persistent, etc.) for a more specific code.

description: Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease with (acute) exacerbation
guideline: Assign during a documented COPD exacerbation. If acute bronchitis is also present, use J44.0. Do not assign both J44.0 and J44.1 for the same episode.
Stage 3 CKD is split into 3a (N18.31) and 3b (N18.32) — use the subcode when GFR staging is documented. Always code the underlying cause (e.g., diabetes, hypertension) as an additional code.
Use for nonspecific lumbago. If sciatica or radiculopathy is present, use M54.4 or M54.3. For herniated disc, use M51.x codes instead.
Use for inflammation of subcutaneous fat not otherwise specified. If the site is specified, use laterality and site subcodes. Query for lupus panniculitis or other specific causes.
Always specify laterality for joint codes. Use M17.12 for left knee, M17.0 for bilateral. Differentiate primary from post-traumatic osteoarthritis (M17.3x).

description: Major depressive disorder, single episode, unspecified
guideline: Assign when severity is not documented. Encourage providers to specify mild (F32.0), moderate (F32.1), or severe without psychosis (F32.2) for accurate coding.
Assign when chronic, excessive worry is documented across multiple domains. Do not confuse with F41.0 (panic disorder) or F40.x (phobia-related disorders).
AUD severity is mild (F10.10), moderate (F10.20), or severe (F10.20 with 4th/5th characters). Document withdrawal status — F10.239 includes withdrawal with unspecified complications.
Use as the primary diagnosis for a vaccination-only visit. Always pair with a procedure code for the specific vaccine administered (e.g., 90688 for flu).

description: Encounter for general adult medical examination without abnormal findings
guideline: Use for annual wellness exams with no abnormal findings. If findings are present, use Z00.01. Cannot be billed same day as a sick visit without modifier -25.
Assign when the sole purpose of the visit is drug level monitoring (e.g., warfarin, lithium, digoxin). Pair with the drug code and the condition being treated.
Always add as a secondary code when anticoagulation therapy is ongoing. Affects care planning, surgical risk documentation, and HCC capture.
Assign when elevated cholesterol or dyslipidemia is documented without type specification. Use E78.00 for pure hypercholesterolemia or E78.1 for pure hypertriglyceridemia if specified.
Use when BMI is ≥30 but a specific obesity type is not documented. Pair with a BMI code (Z68.x) to support risk stratification and HCC coding.

description: Urinary tract infection, site not specified
guideline: Use when the site is not documented. Pair with B96.x to identify the causative organism if culture results are available.
For generic URI/common cold. Avoid if sinusitis, pharyngitis, or laryngitis is specifically documented — more precise codes exist for each.
Assign when GERD with documented esophageal inflammation or erosion is present. Use K21.9 if no esophagitis is noted. Barrett's esophagus requires K22.70–K22.719.
Low to moderate complexity MDM. Requires at least 2 of 3 key components: expanded problem-focused history, exam, and low-complexity MDM.
Moderate complexity MDM. Used when detailed history, physical exam, and moderate-complexity MDM are all documented.
High complexity MDM. Reserved for patients with multiple chronic conditions, severe exacerbations, or significant new problems requiring extensive workup.
Low complexity MDM for new patients. All 3 key components must be met for new patient visits (history, exam, MDM), unlike established patient visits.
Moderate complexity MDM for new patients. Document all 3 key components. Time-based billing requires documentation of total face-to-face time.
Annual wellness exam. Cannot be billed same day as a problem-focused E&M without modifier -25 and separate documentation of a distinct, significant service.
Same rules as 99395. Code selection is age-driven, not complexity-driven. Document all preventive services rendered (counseling, screenings, immunizations).

description: Electrocardiogram (ECG), routine, with interpretation and report
guideline: Bill when the provider personally interprets and documents findings. Use 93005 for tracing only (technical component) or 93010 for interpretation only (professional component).
Report when both PA and lateral views are obtained. Use 71045 for a single view. The interpreting provider's documented findings are required for billing.
Includes 14 tests (glucose, BUN, creatinine, electrolytes, liver enzymes, etc.). Do not unbundle individual tests when a panel is ordered. Must be ordered by a treating provider.
Includes automated differential. Use 85027 for CBC without differential. Document clinical indication to support medical necessity for payer review.
For individual psychotherapy sessions of 53+ minutes. Use 90832 (30 min) or 90834 (45 min) for shorter sessions. Can be billed with E&M using add-on code 90833/90836.

description: Therapeutic, prophylactic, or diagnostic injection, subcutaneous or intramuscular
guideline: Use for IM or SQ injections (e.g., B12, ketorolac). Bill separately from the office visit E&M. Pair with the drug J-code for the substance administered.

description: Collection of venous blood by venipuncture
guideline: Report for routine blood draws. Most payers reimburse only when performed in a non-laboratory office setting. Not separately billable in many facility settings.

description: Aspiration and/or injection of major joint or bursa
guideline: Use for knee, shoulder, hip joint injections/aspirations. Use 20611 when ultrasound guidance is performed. Document joint, substance injected, and laterality.
Medicare-specific code for AWV after the initial visit (G0438). Cannot be billed within 12 months of a previous AWV. Includes health risk assessment and personalized prevention plan.
First AWV under Medicare — patient must have had Part B for at least 12 months. Distinct from the Welcome to Medicare visit (G0402). Documents baseline health status.
Medicare administration code — always pair with the appropriate vaccine product code (e.g., 90688). No beneficiary cost-share applies for this preventive service.
Pair with the pneumococcal vaccine product code (90670 for PCV15/PCV20, 90732 for PPSV23). Medicare covers both doses when appropriately spaced and documented.

description: Visit complexity inherent to evaluation and management associated with primary care
guideline: Add-on code reportable with most E&M visit codes for primary care providers. Recognizes the complexity of longitudinal, continuous care. Cannot be used with AWV or preventive codes.
Report the number of units based on the dose administered (e.g., 1g = 4 units). Pair with 96372 for the injection administration. Document indication and dose in the note.

description: Injection, vitamin B-12 cyanocobalamin, up to 1000 mcg
guideline: One unit = up to 1000 mcg. Pair with 96372 for IM injection. Document deficiency diagnosis (E53.8) or pernicious anemia (D51.0) to support medical necessity.

description: Major joint replacement or reattachment of lower extremity without MCC
guideline: Highest-volume MS-DRG. Accurate comorbidity documentation can shift the case to DRG 469 (with MCC), significantly impacting reimbursement. Query for malnutrition, anemia, and renal status.

description: Heart failure and shock with MCC
guideline: Requires documented HF type (systolic vs. diastolic, acute vs. chronic) and a major complication/comorbidity (MCC). Query providers for specificity — vague 'CHF' maps to lower-weighted DRGs.

description: Simple pneumonia and pleurisy with MCC
guideline: Documentation of the causative organism (e.g., Streptococcus, Klebsiella) and MCC presence drives DRG assignment. Unspecified pneumonia (J18.9) may be queried for pathogen specificity.

description: Esophagitis, gastroenteritis, and miscellaneous digestive disorders without MCC
guideline: Often assigned for GI admissions without a clear surgical procedure. Query for Crohn's, C. diff (A04.72), or other specific diagnoses that may shift to a higher-weighted DRG.
